report_name: Taiwan's Government Issues Warning on Cybersecurity Risks of Chinese Mobile Apps

threat_actors.name: Chinese Government
threat_actors.description: The Chinese government is identified as a national security threat due to its national security laws (Cybersecurity Law and National Intelligence Law) that could compel Chinese app developers to surrender data on Taiwanese users.
threat_actors.goals: Collect intelligence on Taiwanese users, including population movements, social networks, and public sentiment.

attack_patterns.name: Location Tracking
attack_patterns.description: The Amap application was found to perform continuous location tracking, even when the app was closed.

attack_patterns.name: Audio Capture
attack_patterns.description: The Amap application requested and used access to the device's microphone.

attack_patterns.name: Protected User Data
attack_patterns.description: The Amap application requested and used access to the device's contact list.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The Amap application requested and used access to the device's media files.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The analyzed applications, including Amap, bilibili, iQIYI, and BIMOBIMO, transmit collected user data to servers located in China.

malware.name: Amap
malware.description: A Chinese-made navigation app identified by Taiwan's MODA for aggressive and excessive data collection, including location, contacts, media, and microphone data, which is transmitted to servers in China.

malware.name: bilibili
malware.description: A Chinese-made video streaming app identified by Taiwan's MODA for requesting excessive permissions and collecting user data, which is transmitted to servers in China.

malware.name: iQIYI
malware.description: A Chinese-made video streaming app identified by Taiwan's MODA for requesting excessive permissions and collecting user data, which is transmitted to servers in China.

malware.name: BIMOBIMO
malware.description: A Chinese-made messaging app identified by Taiwan's MODA for requesting excessive permissions and collecting user data, which is transmitted to servers in China.

identities.name: Taiwan
identities.description: The nation of Taiwan, whose citizens and national security are targeted by the data collection activities of the specified Chinese mobile applications.

infrastructure.name: Data Collection Servers in China
infrastructure.description: Servers located in China that receive user data transmitted from the Amap, bilibili, iQIYI, and BIMOBIMO mobile applications.

indicators.name: Detection of Potentially Unwanted Chinese-Made Applications
indicators.pattern:
title: Detection of Potentially Unwanted Chinese-Made Applications
id: 2b9c1a8e-5d7f-4b1a-9c8d-3e4f5a6b7c8d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of specific Chinese-made mobile applications flagged by Taiwan's MODA for excessive data collection and privacy risks.
references:
  - https://focustaiwan.tw/sci-tech/202605270014
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-27
tags:
  - attack.t1041
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: android
detection:
  selection:
    Image|contains:
      - 'Amap'
      - 'bilibili'
      - 'iQIYI'
      - 'BIMOBIMO'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This rule detects the presence of Chinese-made applications (Amap, bilibili, iQIYI, BIMOBIMO) that have been identified as high-risk for data collection and transmission to servers in China.

relationships.source_name: Detection of Potentially Unwanted Chinese-Made Applications
